Title: Exploiting Natural Variation in Tomato to Define Pathway Structure and Metabolic Regulation of Fruit Polyphenolics in the Lycopersicum Complex

Pathway_Genes.Variety: M82; MicroTom (validation); Arabidopsis Col-0 used for heterologous overexpression
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct glycosylated flavonol products and precursors for more decorated flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to the 3-O position of flavonols such as quercetin to form flavonol-3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and health-beneficial polyphenols; implicated broadly in UV, cold, drought, disease and pest tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predicted/observed active across tomato tissues and species; function validated in tomato fruit and Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key decoration step; bottleneck for 3-O-glucosylated flavonols in validated context
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for Arabidopsis overexpression; pTRV2-based VIGS construct in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3-O-glucosides; silencing lowers and overexpression increases these metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; GC-MS metabolomics; TOM2 microarray; public RNA-seq (Koenig et al. 2013; Bolger et al. 2014)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein assay; Arabidopsis overexpression/complementation-like assay; tomato transient overexpression; tomato VIGS; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g083440 was annotated as the tomato flavonoid-3-O-glucosyltransferase candidate within the refined flavonoid pathway. 2. Recombinant protein assay confirmed glycosyltransferase activity on flavonol substrate(s). 3. Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased flavonol-3Glc-related compounds, whereas VIGS suppression in tomato fruit reduced flavonol-3-O-glucosides and transient overexpression increased them.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as SlUGT78D-a / SlUGT78D1-a / SlF3GlcT; conserved PSPG motif among several tomato species was reported, supporting conserved 3-O-glycosylation function.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: MicroTom for VIGS/transient assays; pathway annotation based on LYCO and wild accessions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms 7-O-glucosylated flavonol products downstream of flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively transfers glucose to the 7-O position of flavonoid/flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonol glycosides such as Q3Glc6''Rha-related intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 7-O-glucosylated flavonol derivatives such as Q3Glc6''Rha7Glc-related products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic antioxidants with potential protective functions in plants and nutritional relevance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and fruits in species showing flavonol-7-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predicted absent/non-expressed in PENN where flavonol-7-O-glucosides were undetected; candidate expressed in HABR seedlings/leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Species- and tissue-specific accumulation; fruit ripening context
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: pTRV2-based VIGS construct for tomato fruit transient silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes accumulation of flavonol-7-O-glucosylated products, but evidence remains preliminary
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; TOM2 microarray; public RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; in silico expression analysis; tomato transient overexpression/VIGS with LC-MS readout
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g079350 was proposed as a candidate tomato flavonoid-7-O-glucosyltransferase based on pathway structure, expression pattern, and phylogeny. 2. Species lacking flavonol-7-O-glucosides were predicted to lack expression/activity of this step. 3. Functional evidence was suggestive but not definitive, and the authors state that further characterization is required.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes sequence divergence from known F7GT clades and explicitly states that further characterization is required; therefore assignment is candidate-level rather than confirmed.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: M82/reference LYCO; cross-species comparison included PIMP, HABR, PENN and others
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts flavonol-3-O-glucosides to apiosylated derivatives that feed higher-order flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively transfers apiose onto flavonol-3-O-glucoside at the 2'' position
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and fruits in species accumulating flavonol-3Glc2''Api derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in HABR leaves according to candidate analysis; predicted absent or not expressed in CHMI and NEOR because these species lacked flavonol-3Glc2''Api in fruits and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of apiosylated flavonol derivatives and associated higher-order flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; public RNA-seq; phylogenetic analysis of tomato UGTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite presence/absence inference; phylogenetic analysis; expression-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc10g008860 was identified as the candidate gene for the flavonol-3Glc-2''-O-apiosyltransferase step. 2. Its expression pattern and phylogenetic placement were consistent with further decoration of flavonol-3-O-Glc. 3. Lack of the corresponding metabolites in CHMI and NEOR suggested absence or non-expression of this gene in those species.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is inferred from comparative metabolomics and UGT phylogeny, not directly biochemically validated in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: M82/reference LYCO; cross-species analysis across wild accessions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates rutinoside/rhamnosylated flavonol intermediates leading to abundant tomato flavonol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively transfers rhamnose to the 6'' position of flavonoid/flavonol 3-O-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Lower expression inferred in HABR and higher in PENN from in silico transcriptome analysis; low HABR expression correlated with low kaempferol/quercetin-3Glc6''Rha accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key decoration step influencing rutinoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of flavonol-3Glc6''Rha derivatives; reduced expression associates with lower corresponding metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression-metabolite correlation; comparative metabolomics; ortholog annotation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc09g059170 was annotated as the candidate flavonoid-3-O-glc-6''-O-rhamnosyltransferase. 2. Lower expression in HABR correlated with low accumulation of kaempferol/quercetin-3Glc6''Rha and related isorhamnetin derivatives. 3. In silico transcriptomic data supported species-dependent expression differences consistent with metabolite patterns.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment is based on orthology and metabolite/transcript correlation rather than direct enzyme assay in this paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts quercetin derivatives toward methylated flavonols such as isorhamnetin glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates hydroxylated flavonol substrates to form methylated quercetin/isorhamnetin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and fruits; notably high in HABR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Inferred from high isorhamnetin accumulation, especially in HABR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of methylated flavonol derivatives (isorhamnetin glycosides)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc03g080180 was annotated as OMT1 in the refined tomato flavonoid pathway. 2. The pathway placement explains production of methylated quercetin derivatives annotated as isorhamnetin glycosides. 3. High isorhamnetin accumulation in HABR is consistent with strong flux through this methylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly validated biochemically in this paper; functional assignment relies on orthology and metabolite structures.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT71B-a-related candidate near C-glycosyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the dihydrochalcone glycoside P35diGlc
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C-glycosylation of phloretin to produce phloretin-3',5'-C-di-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized polyphenolic/dihydrochalcone metabolite with potential protective and nutritional relevance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature fruits of red-fruited species and PERU/HABR
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Inferred from presence of P35diGlc in species/metabolite datasets
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Direct product-forming step for this branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of P35diGlc in the tomato polyphenol network
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS metabolomics; phylogenetic analysis of UGTs; ortholog search
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc02g088500 was identified as the candidate tomato ortholog for phloretin-3',5'-C-diglucosyltransferase. 2. Phylogenetically it clustered near flavonoid-C-glucosyltransferases from rice and citrus. 3. The assignment explains formation of P35diGlc within the reconstructed tomato polyphenol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains some alias inconsistency for UGT naming; functional assignment as P3'5'CGT is candidate-level.
